hero.description: Fight through a dark Edo-period Kyoto as Miyamoto Musashi, mastering parries, Issen strikes, Oni Gauntlet powers, and brutal boss duels against the Genma.

onimushaReleaseDatePlatforms.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Release Date and Platforms
onimushaReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: Use this release snapshot to check launch timing, supported platforms, store coverage, and the current PC hardware targets before you pre-order or wishlist the game.

rows.label: Release Date
rows.details: Capcom confirmed the full launch date for the game.

rows.label: Platforms
rows.value: PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC
rows.details: Console and Windows PC versions are listed across the main official storefronts.

rows.label: PC Stores
rows.value: Steam, Epic Games Store, and Xbox PC
rows.details: PC players can wishlist or pre-order through their preferred store ecosystem.

rows.label: Player Mode
rows.details: The current listings focus on a solo dark fantasy action campaign.

rows.label: Minimum PC Spec
rows.value: Core i5-8400 / Ryzen 3 3100, 16GB RAM, GTX 1660 or RX 5500 XT, 50GB SSD
rows.details: The current Windows requirements target modern hardware with SSD storage.

rows.label: Recommended PC Spec
rows.value: Core i5-10400 / Ryzen 5 3600, 16GB RAM, RTX 2060 Super or RX 6600, 50GB SSD
rows.details: Recommended hardware is aimed at a smoother combat presentation during large encounters.

onimushaDemoDownloadRewards.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Demo Download and Rewards
onimushaDemoDownloadRewards.intro: The free demo is the fastest way to test the pace of combat, the feel of Musashi's defense timing, and the first reward track tied to save data.

cards.title: Where to Download
cards.description: The demo is available through PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, Epic Games Store, and the Steam store page.

cards.title: What You Play
cards.description: The demo runs for about 30 minutes and includes the Kiyomizu-dera route, early combat encounters, and the Sasaki Ganryu boss fight.

cards.title: Reward for Save Data
cards.description: Demo save data unlocks Charm: Kubi Akari in the full release.

cards.title: Carryover Rule
cards.description: Your progress does not transfer into the full game, but the save file still matters for the charm reward.

onimushaPreOrderBonusesEditions.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Pre-Order Bonuses and Editions
onimushaPreOrderBonusesEditions.intro: Choose between the Standard, Deluxe, and Premium Deluxe options based on whether you want gameplay charms only or the broader cosmetic bundle that expands Musashi and ally customization.

editions.name: Standard Edition
editions.summary: Base game plus the core pre-order reward pack.

editions.name: Deluxe Edition
editions.summary: Adds the Deluxe Kit with extra charms, a Musashi outfit, Oni Gauntlet styling, and multiple sword appearances.
includes: Deluxe Kit cosmetics and bonus charms

editions.name: Premium Deluxe Edition
editions.summary: Expands the cosmetic set further with additional haori looks, ally outfits, and the mini digital soundtrack.

onimushaBeginnerGuide.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Beginner Guide
onimushaBeginnerGuide.intro: This beginner guide turns the official demo systems into a simple first-play route. Start by reading enemy attacks, use parry and deflect to create openings, absorb souls when safe, and save Oni Armament for high-pressure moments.

steps.title: Play the demo first
steps.summary: The demo covers around 30 minutes of the early story and lets players try Parry, Deflect, Issen, Oni Armament, soul absorption, and the Kiyomizu-dera battle against Sasaki Ganryu.
steps.action: Use the demo as a combat tutorial instead of rushing straight into the boss.
steps.demoNote: Demo progress does not carry over, but having demo save data grants Charm: Kubi Akari in the full game.

steps.title: Move before you attack
steps.summary: Musashi can dodge enemy pressure, and repeated dodging fills the Reflex Dodge gauge for a Reflex Combo.
steps.action: Circle enemies, dodge repeated attacks, and watch for safe openings before committing to long attack strings.
steps.demoNote: Reflex Combo is best treated as a reward for staying alive and reading enemy patterns.

steps.title: Use Parry and Deflect as your base defense
steps.summary: Onimusha: Way of the Sword centers blade-on-blade combat around parrying and deflecting, giving fights a strong timing rhythm.
steps.action: Block less by habit and focus on reacting to the enemy swing, then counter when the clash creates space.
steps.demoNote: The Kiyomizu-dera demo is designed to teach this rhythm before the Sasaki Ganryu duel.

steps.title: Look for Issen timing
steps.summary: Issen is the series' signature critical attack and returns as a high-impact technique tied to clean timing.
steps.action: Do not spam it. Wait for a clear enemy commitment, then use the opening created by a strong defensive read.
steps.demoNote: Use smaller enemies to practice the timing before relying on it against Sasaki Ganryu.

steps.title: Absorb souls safely
steps.summary: The Oni Gauntlet can absorb Genma souls and convert them into power that enhances Musashi.
steps.action: Absorb souls after an enemy is down, after a clean counter, or when there is enough distance from the next attack.
steps.demoNote: Soul absorption is part of the core combat loop, not just a reward animation.

steps.title: Save Oni Armament for pressure moments
steps.summary: The demo lets players use Oni Armament attacks as part of Musashi's supernatural swordplay kit.
steps.action: Use Oni Armament when a tougher enemy is pressuring you, when a boss opening appears, or when you need a strong burst of damage.
steps.demoNote: Oni Armament is especially useful when normal sword pressure is not enough.

steps.title: Learn Oni Strength and Oni Agility
steps.summary: Oni Strength helps break through stalemates and enemy defenses, while Oni Agility gives Musashi explosive movement and can help him access new areas.
steps.action: Use Oni Strength when an enemy is guarding hard, and use Oni Agility when the route asks for fast movement or wall traversal.
steps.demoNote: These powers matter for both combat and exploration.

steps.title: Treat Sasaki Ganryu as the first real exam
steps.summary: Sasaki Ganryu appears as Musashi's arch-rival and the formidable boss of the Kiyomizu-dera demo.
steps.action: Focus on reading his attacks, surviving the first phase, and using parry, deflect, Issen, and Oni Armament only when the timing is clear.
steps.demoNote: The goal is not only to win, but to understand the full combat loop.

onimushaBeginnerGuide.quickTipsTitle: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Quick Tips
quickTips: Use the demo as a safe place to build timing habits before launch day.
Short strings and clean resets are better than greedy pressure in the temple route.
Practice parry and deflect on smaller enemies before forcing Issen against the boss.
Save Oni Armament for moments where regular swordplay stops creating space.

onimushaCombatOniGauntlet.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Combat and Oni Gauntlet
onimushaCombatOniGauntlet.intro: Combat in Onimusha: Way of the Sword is built around sharp sword clashes, supernatural Oni power, and the rhythm of creating openings. The Oni Gauntlet is not only a story artifact: it powers Musashi's offense, defense, exploration, and growth.

mechanics.name: Parry
mechanics.summary: A core defensive action used in blade-on-blade combat.
mechanics.details: Parry helps Musashi meet enemy attacks directly and turn pressure into a counterattack opportunity.
mechanics.useCase: Use it when an enemy commits to a clear melee strike.

mechanics.name: Deflect
mechanics.summary: A timing-based defensive tool that works alongside parry.
mechanics.details: Deflect supports the game's tactical sword clashes and helps players survive aggressive enemy patterns.
mechanics.useCase: Use it to interrupt pressure and reset the fight in your favor.

mechanics.name: Issen
mechanics.details: Issen rewards strong timing and creates one of the game's biggest combat payoffs after a clean read.
mechanics.useCase: Use it after learning an enemy's attack rhythm instead of forcing it blindly.

mechanics.name: Soul Absorption
mechanics.summary: The Oni Gauntlet absorbs Genma souls and converts them into power.
mechanics.details: Soul absorption connects enemy defeat to character power, making it a key part of Musashi's combat loop.
mechanics.useCase: Absorb after a safe opening, a knockdown, or a finished exchange.

mechanics.name: Oni Armament
mechanics.summary: A supernatural attack option available in the demo.
mechanics.details: Oni Armament gives Musashi a heavier burst tool when normal swordplay needs extra force.
mechanics.useCase: Use it against stronger enemies, boss openings, or moments where pressure is too high.

mechanics.name: Oni Strength
mechanics.summary: An Oni power that breaks through stalemates with brute force.
mechanics.details: Oni Strength can shatter enemy defenses and also supports exploration by breaking through obstacles.
mechanics.useCase: Use it when an enemy refuses to open up or when the route requires force.

mechanics.name: Oni Agility
mechanics.summary: An Oni power focused on explosive movement.
mechanics.details: Oni Agility helps Musashi clear paths quickly and can let him run across certain walls to reach new areas.
mechanics.useCase: Use it for fast repositioning and traversal-heavy sections.

mechanics.name: Reflex Combo
mechanics.summary: A powerful combo tied to the Reflex Dodge gauge.
mechanics.details: Continuously dodging enemy attacks fills the Reflex Dodge gauge, and once filled, Musashi can unleash a Reflex Combo.
mechanics.useCase: Use it after surviving repeated pressure through clean dodges.

onimushaCombatOniGauntlet.combatLoopTitle: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Suggested Combat Loop
combatLoop: Bait a clear enemy swing and stay ready to react instead of opening every exchange first.
Answer with parry or deflect to break the enemy's tempo and create a safe window.
Convert the opening into Issen, a short sword string, or Oni Armament if pressure is high.
Absorb souls only when the arena gives you enough distance to reset for the next threat.

onimushaCharactersStory.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Characters and Story
onimushaCharactersStory.intro: Onimusha: Way of the Sword follows Miyamoto Musashi through a dark fantasy version of early Edo-period Kyoto. The city is twisted by Malice, Genma run rampant, and the Oni Gauntlet pulls Musashi into battles that test both his sword and his reason to fight.

characters.name: Miyamoto Musashi
characters.description: An ardent samurai who arrives in Kyoto looking to prove he is unmatched in the way of the sword, before his path takes a strange turn.
keyFacts: Face model based on Toshiro Mifune

characters.name: Oni Gauntlet
characters.description: A powerful artifact that gives Musashi strength beyond human limits and the ability to fight Genma.

characters.name: Sasaki Ganryu
characters.description: Musashi's arch-rival and the major opponent players face in the Kiyomizu-dera demo battle.
keyFacts: Designed as a major timing test

characters.name: Genma
characters.description: Monstrous beings from the underworld whose arrival has plunged Kyoto into dread and panic.
keyFacts: Brought into the living world by Malice
Serve as Musashi's main supernatural enemy
Can be cut down with the Oni Gauntlet's power

characters.name: Edo-period Kyoto
characters.description: The historic Japanese capital appears as a dark fantasy battlefield filled with shrines, temples, danger, and mystery.
keyFacts: Twisted by malevolent clouds of Malice
Includes locations such as Kiyomizu-dera Temple
Combines history, folklore, and dark fantasy

characters.name: Musashi's Reason to Fight
characters.description: The story follows Musashi as he searches for his reason to fight while facing Genma, rivals, and the burden of Oni power.
keyFacts: Focuses on gritty combat and character drive
Uses the Oni Gauntlet as both power and mystery
Built as a new entry in the Onimusha series

onimushaBossesGenmaLocations.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Bosses, Genma, and Locations
onimushaBossesGenmaLocations.intro: This section tracks the enemies and locations already shown through official pages, trailers, and demo information. It focuses on confirmed names and roles so players can understand the world without story spoilers.

entries.name: Genma
entries.description: Monstrosities from the underworld and the main supernatural threat in Onimusha: Way of the Sword.
entries.knownContext: The Malice enveloping Kyoto brings the Genma into the world of the living, causing dread and panic across the capital.
entries.playerFocus: Learn enemy attack patterns, then use parry, deflect, Issen, and soul absorption to control fights.

entries.name: Sasaki Ganryu
entries.description: A major rival opponent featured in the official demo.
entries.knownContext: Players face Sasaki Ganryu during the Kiyomizu-dera Temple stage in the early-story demo.
entries.playerFocus: Treat the duel as a test of defensive timing, Issen openings, and smart Oni Armament use.

entries.name: Shuten Doji
entries.description: A colossal Genma connected to Mount Oe.
entries.knownContext: Shuten Doji uses Mount Oe as his stronghold and claims to be the Oni of legend.
entries.playerFocus: Expect a large-scale fight built around reading heavy attacks and surviving supernatural pressure.

entries.name: Mount Oe
entries.description: A famous mountain area in Kyoto where natural scenery, history, and legend meet.
entries.knownContext: In Onimusha: Way of the Sword, Mount Oe has been taken over by a Genma who waits for Musashi.
entries.playerFocus: Watch for wide battle spaces, environmental storytelling, and encounters tied to Shuten Doji.

entries.name: Kiyomizu-dera Temple
entries.description: A major Kyoto temple location used as the official demo stage.
entries.knownContext: The demo lets players step into the Kiyomizu-dera Temple stage and battle Sasaki Ganryu.
entries.playerFocus: Use this area to learn movement, parry, deflect, Issen, soul absorption, and Oni Armament.

entries.name: Edo-period Kyoto
entries.description: The historic capital is transformed into a dark fantasy battlefield by malevolent clouds of Malice.
entries.knownContext: Official descriptions highlight shrines, temples, bloodstained battlefields, mystery, danger, and Genma-infested stages.
entries.playerFocus: Explore carefully, watch for Oni power traversal routes, and expect each stage to mix combat with atmosphere.

entries.name: Formidable Foes
entries.description: Powerful opponents whose martial prowess can rival Musashi's own sword skill.
entries.knownContext: Official store pages describe bloody duels to the death against strong opponents in the capital.
entries.playerFocus: Approach these fights with patience: read the attack rhythm first, then punish with Issen or Oni-powered options.

faq.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about the release, demo, combat structure, and platform support for Onimusha: Way of the Sword.

questions.question: When does Onimusha: Way of the Sword release?
questions.answer: Onimusha: Way of the Sword launches on September 25, 2026.

questions.question: Where can I play Onimusha: Way of the Sword?
questions.answer: Onimusha: Way of the Sword is confirmed for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Windows PC.

questions.question: Does Onimusha: Way of the Sword have a demo?
questions.answer: Yes. A playable demo is available now and includes early combat, the Kiyomizu-dera route, and the Sasaki Ganryu fight.

questions.question: Does Onimusha: Way of the Sword demo carry progress into launch?
questions.answer: Demo progress does not transfer into the full release, but demo save data can unlock Charm: Kubi Akari.

questions.question: Is Onimusha: Way of the Sword single-player only?
questions.answer: The current official listings present Onimusha: Way of the Sword as a single-player swordplay action game.

questions.question: What makes Onimusha: Way of the Sword combat stand out?
questions.answer: The combat focus is built around parry, deflect, Issen timing, soul absorption, and the Oni Gauntlet's powered techniques.
